html:
[202]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • ○食核桃 - Title meaning '○ eating hawthorn nuts.' The '○' symbol is often used in classical texts as a placeholder or to indicate a section break.
  • 留后 - Military or administrative position in the Tang and Song dynasties, roughly equivalent to a deputy governor or military commander.
  • 佣赁者 - Refers to a hired worker or laborer.
  • 役之既久 - Means 'after having served for a long time.'
  • 可容数升 - Means 'can hold several liters,' describing the size of the hawthorn nut.
  • 稍磨之取食 - Means 'to grind it slightly and eat it.'
  • 颇觉轻健 - Means 'feeling quite light and healthy.'
  • 贪猥残虐 - Means 'greedy, corrupt, and cruel.'
  • 毕师铎之难 - Refers to a historical event involving毕师铎 (Bi Shiduo), a military leader during the Tang dynasty. Likely refers to a crisis or conflict led by毕师铎.
  • 投所居后阁井中死 - Means 'he was thrown into a well in the back of his residence and died.'
  • 求得类尧卿者杀之 - Means 'they sought out someone similar to Yaoqing and killed them.'
  • 窃知其尸在井中 - Means 'they secretly found out that his body was in the well.'
  • 举体皆腐坏 - Means 'the entire body was rotten and decayed.'
  • 藏府有成金者 - Means 'the internal organs contained solid gold.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杨子 - Refers to Yangzi, a historical figure or literary character. The exact identity is unclear without further context.
  • 吴尧卿 - Refers to Wu Yaoqing, likely a historical figure or literary character. The exact identity is unclear without further context.
  • 毕师铎 - A historical figure from the Tang dynasty, known for his military actions and conflicts.
  • 《积神录》 - A historical or literary text, possibly a record of supernatural events or stories. The title '积神录' means 'Record of Accumulated Spirits.'
  • 《广记》 - Refers to the 'Yingzao Fashi' (Building Standard Treatise), a comprehensive encyclopedia compiled during the Song dynasty. The '卷四一○' indicates the specific volume and section.

Proper Nouns

  • 杨子 - A historical figure or literary character.
  • 吴尧卿 - A historical figure or literary character.
  • 毕师铎 - A historical figure from the Tang dynasty.
  • 《积神录》 - A historical or literary text.
  • 《广记》 - The 'Yingzao Fashi' (Building Standard Treatise), a comprehensive encyclopedia compiled during the Song dynasty.

[203]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 掬沙扑人 - Metaphorical expression describing the ghost's action of scooping sand to hit people, which is both unusual and symbolic.
  • 既棺歛 - Means 'after being placed in the coffin and sealed,' indicating the completion of funeral rites.
  • 自门而入，径至殡所 - Describes the ghost's direct and unobstructed entry into the place where the deceased was laid out.
  • 椎声喧噪 - Refers to loud, chaotic sounds, possibly from the ghost's activity or the disturbance it caused.
  • 性犹恍惚 - Means 'still in a dazed or confused state,' describing Fwu's mental condition after revival.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 新安人吴生 - Refers to Wu Sheng, a person from Xin'an (now part of Anhui province), who died and was revived.
  • 阿房宫 - The A-Fang Palace, a grand imperial palace built during the Qin dynasty, symbolizing the might and extravagance of the Qin rulers.
  • 鄱阳山中 - Refers to a mountainous area in Poyang, Jiangxi, where the wood spirit is said to reside.
  • 秦时 - Refers to the Qin dynasty, a period known for its centralized rule and significant construction projects.

Proper Nouns

  • 新安 - A historical region in Anhui province.
  • 吴生 - The name of the person who died and was revived.
  • 阿房宫 - The A-Fang Palace.
  • 鄱阳 - A city in Jiangxi province.
  • 建康 - The capital of the Eastern Jin dynasty, now known as Nanjing.
  • 木客 - The name given to the wood spirit.

[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 干大 - A name or title, possibly a spiritual or legendary figure; meaning is unclear.
  • 樵人 - Literally 'woodcutter,' but here refers to a hermit or recluse living in the mountains.
  • 拱揖而已 - A formal gesture of respect, indicating that the person only bows and does not speak.
  • 俟其出 - Means 'wait for him to come out,' referring to the young man waiting for the woodcutter to appear.
  • 愿事先生 - A respectful way of saying 'I wish to serve you' or 'I wish to become your disciple.'
  • 干大惊欲去 - The woodcutter is surprised and wants to leave.
  • 急捉其衣褐 - The young man grabs the woodcutter's robe (褐) to prevent him from leaving.
  • 经历险绝，辄不踅舍 - The young man follows the woodcutter through dangerous and difficult terrain, never stopping or turning back.
  • 掬水吞之 - The woodcutter takes water from the stream and has the young man drink it.
  • 遂失干大 - The woodcutter disappears, and the young man loses sight of him.
  • 寻樵路而出 - The young man follows the path of the woodcutter and eventually finds his way out.
  • 数日还家 - The young man returns home after several days.
  • 自此轻健无疾 - The young man becomes light, strong, and free of illness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 洪州西山 - Refers to the western mountains of Hongzhou (洪州), an ancient name for Nanchang, Jiangxi Province.
  • 稽神录 - A collection of strange stories and legends, often involving supernatural or spiritual figures.
  • 历世真仙体道通鉴 - A historical record of immortals and their teachings, often used as a source for Daoist and spiritual narratives.

Proper Nouns

  • 干大 - The name or title of the woodcutter, possibly a legendary or spiritual figure; meaning is unclear.
  • 洪州西山 - Refers to the western mountains of Hongzhou (洪州), an ancient name for Nanchang, Jiangxi Province.
  • 分宁县 - Refers to Fenning County (分宁县), a place in Jiangxi Province.
  • 稽神录 - A collection of strange stories and legends, often involving supernatural or spiritual figures.
  • 历世真仙体道通鉴 - A historical record of immortals and their teachings, often used as a source for Daoist and spiritual narratives.
